info.title: Filament DB API
info.description: REST API for managing 3D printing filament profiles, spools, nozzles, printers, bed types, locations, print history, sharing, slicer integrations, and OpenPrintTag imports.

tags.name: Filaments
tags.description: CRUD and bulk operations for filament profiles

tags.name: Bed Types
tags.description: Manage build plate / bed surface definitions

tags.name: PrusaSlicer
tags.description: PrusaSlicer config bundle export and import

tags.name: OpenPrintTag
tags.description: OpenPrintTag community database browser and import

tags.name: Scan Stream
tags.description: Live NFC scan fan-out via Server-Sent Events

tags.name: Sharing
tags.description: Publish and retrieve shared filament catalogs

get.description: Returns all non-deleted filaments, optionally filtered by type, vendor, or search term.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by filament type (e.g. PLA, PETG)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive regex search on filament name

200.description: Array of filament summaries sorted by name. This is an aggregation projection (FilamentSummary), NOT the full Filament document — heavy spool subfields are dropped, temperatures is reduced to nozzle+bed, and two computed booleans (hasCalibrations, hasVariants) are added. Use GET /api/filaments/{id} for the full document.

post.description: Create a new filament profile. If parentId is provided, the filament becomes a color variant inheriting settings from its parent. Alternatively, send { tagData, overrides } where tagData is a DecodedOpenPrintTag from POST /api/nfc/decode: the server maps the decoded tag to a filament payload (mobile create-from-scan, plan §4.4), then overrides win over the mapped fields — the scanner uses this to set the user-edited name/vendor/type (which are required). Spool subdocuments are never fabricated on create.

get.description: Returns a single filament with resolved inherited values (for variants), populated nozzle/printer references, and any child variants. Pass `?raw=true` to skip parent-inheritance resolution and receive only the variant's own overrides — used by the edit page to avoid silently copying parent values onto the child (GH #106). When `?raw=true` is passed on a variant the response includes a `_parent` summary projection.

parameters.name: raw
parameters.description: When 'true', skip parent-inheritance resolution and return only the variant's own field values. Fields the variant doesn't override come back as null. Required by the variant edit form so saving doesn't copy parent values onto the child.

color.description: Primary color hex. May be null for coextruded variants — colors are then in secondaryColors.

secondaryColors.description: Up to 5 additional color slots in display order, mirroring the top-level Filament.secondaryColors. Inherits from parent when the variant declares no own array.

optTags.description: OpenPrintTag tag IDs. Includes the color-arrangement tags (28 = gradual_color_change, 29 = coextruded) so client chips can render the right swatch shape.

_inherited.description: List of field names inherited from parent

put.description: Update filament fields. Validates parentId references to prevent circular or nested inheritance.

post.description: Sync a filament preset back from PrusaSlicer. The {id} param can be a URL-encoded preset name or a MongoDB ObjectId. Reverse-maps PrusaSlicer INI keys to structured DB fields. When nozzle_diameter and high_flow are provided, calibration-related keys (extrusion_multiplier, pressure_advance, retract settings) are written to the matching per-nozzle calibration entry.

parameters.name: nozzle_diameter
parameters.description: Active nozzle diameter in mm. When provided, calibration keys are written to the matching per-nozzle calibration entry.

parameters.name: high_flow
parameters.description: Whether the nozzle is high-flow. Disambiguates same-diameter nozzles (e.g. 0.4mm vs 0.4mm HF).

config.description: PrusaSlicer INI key-value pairs to sync back (e.g. filament_type, temperature, bed_temperature)

message.example: Synced 12 settings for "Prusament PLA Galaxy Black"

delete.description: By default this is a soft delete that sets _deletedAt — the filament moves to the trash and can be restored via POST /api/filaments/{id}/restore. Pass ?permanent=true to mark the row as purged (sets _purged so the hybrid sync engine propagates the deletion to peers); the filament must already be in the trash. Cannot delete a parent that still has variants.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When 'true', permanently purges a trashed filament instead of soft-deleting. The filament must already be in the trash (i.e. previously soft-deleted).

400.description: Cannot delete — has variants, or permanent delete attempted on a filament that isn't in the trash

get.description: Returns soft-deleted filaments (those with a non-null _deletedAt), sorted by deletion time descending. Permanently-purged tombstones (_purged: true) are excluded — they remain in the collection only so the hybrid sync engine can propagate the purge to peers and should never reappear in the trash UI.

color.description: Primary color hex. May be null for coextruded filaments — see Filament.secondaryColors.

secondaryColors.description: Up to 5 additional color slots, mirroring Filament.secondaryColors.

optTags.description: OpenPrintTag tag IDs. Includes the color-arrangement tags (28 = gradual_color_change, 29 = coextruded) so client swatches can render stripes vs gradient vs solid for trashed multi-color filaments.

post.description: Un-soft-deletes a trashed filament by clearing _deletedAt. Refuses with 409 if another active filament now uses the same name — the partial unique index on `name` only covers non-deleted documents, so the caller has to rename one or the other before the restore can succeed.

404.description: Filament not in trash (either does not exist, was never soft-deleted, or has already been permanently purged)

409.description: Name conflict — another active filament already uses this name

get.description: Generates a CBOR-encoded OpenPrintTag binary for NFC writing. Contains filament name, brand, type, color, density, diameter, and temperatures.

Compares an OpenPrintTag-linked filament (one carrying settings.openprinttag_slug) against the current upstream material and returns a field-level changelist. Read-only. `changes[].kind` is `adopt` (field unset / gray sentinel / unchanged from what OPT last wrote — safe) or `conflict` (locally edited away from OPT — surfaced, not auto-applied). Only color, secondaryColors, density, the OPT-carried temperatures, drying temp/time, Shore D, transmission distance and tags are compared; identity fields and diameter are never re-synced.

found.description: false when the slug is gone upstream

post.description: Applies the user-accepted subset of OpenPrintTag updates to a linked filament. Same-origin guarded. Only managed field keys are accepted (unknown keys → 400). Refreshes openprinttagSnapshot to the full current OPT offer so a later check can distinguish upstream changes from local edits on declined fields.

fields.description: Managed field keys to adopt, e.g. "density", "temperatures.nozzle".

400.description: Malformed body, unknown field, a field OpenPrintTag isn't currently offering, or filament not OpenPrintTag-linked

404.description: Filament or upstream material not found

get.description: Returns calibration data for a specific filament and nozzle diameter. Looks up the filament by URL-encoded name or ObjectId, resolves variant inheritance, then finds the calibration entry whose nozzle diameter matches the query param. Used by PrusaSlicer to auto-adjust filament settings when the user switches printer presets.

parameters.name: high_flow
parameters.description: Filter by high-flow nozzle flag. Disambiguates same-diameter nozzles (e.g. 0.4mm vs 0.4mm HF).

200.description: Calibration data for the matched nozzle

printer.description: Printer name, or null if calibration is not printer-specific

nozzleTemp.description: Recommended nozzle temperature in °C (GH #514.5 — Codex follow-up on PR #542; the GET /calibration handler returns these alongside the original six)

404.description: Filament not found or no calibration for that nozzle diameter

available.description: List of nozzle diameters that do have calibration data

get.summary: Check spool sufficiency for a print job
get.description: Checks whether any spool of this filament has enough remaining filament (by weight in grams) for a print job. If no spools or no spool weight data exists, returns ok: true (no data = no warning). Used by PrusaSlicer to warn after slicing if insufficient filament.

parameters.name: weight
parameters.description: Estimated filament weight in grams for the print job

ok.description: True if at least one spool has enough remaining filament

requiredLengthM.description: Required length in meters (null if density/diameter unavailable)

warning.description: Human-readable warning when ok is false

get.description: Exports all filaments as a PrusaSlicer-compatible INI config bundle. Generates one [filament:Name] section per filament with base settings; nozzle-specific calibration data is applied dynamically by PrusaSlicer via the per-filament calibration endpoint.

schema.example:
# PrusaSlicer config bundle generated by Filament DB

[filament:Prusament PLA]
extrusion_multiplier = 0.95

post.description: Imports filament profiles from an uploaded INI file. Creates new filaments or updates existing ones matched by name.

400.description: No file provided or no profiles found

post.description: Connect to a remote MongoDB Atlas instance to list or import filaments. Send only `uri` to list available filaments; send `uri` + `filamentIds` to import selected ones.

filamentIds.description: IDs of filaments to import. Omit to list all available filaments.

200.description: List of remote filaments (no filamentIds) or import result (with filamentIds)

color.description: Primary color hex from the Atlas source. May be null for coextruded filaments.

post.summary: Decode raw NFC tag bytes and match against the database
post.description: Decodes an OpenPrintTag (ISO 15693 / NFC-V, CBOR) or Bambu Lab (MIFARE Classic) tag from raw bytes a client read on the phone — the mobile scanner app's primary call — and attaches a confident DB match using the same priority order as /api/filaments/match. Decoding runs server-side so clients ship no decoder (the Bambu path needs Node crypto in particular). Read-only: performs no mutation and, like /api/filaments/match, is intentionally NOT behind the same-origin CSRF guard so cross-origin clients can reach it. When FILAMENTDB_API_KEY is set, every /api caller must present the bearer key.

payload.description: OpenPrintTag only: base64 of the NDEF record payload (CBOR). Preferred — iOS Core NFC returns parsed NDEF records. Supply this OR tagMemory.

tagMemory.description: OpenPrintTag only: base64 of raw tag memory; the server runs the NDEF parser to extract the CBOR payload. Supply this OR payload.

blocks.description: Bambu only: map of MIFARE block number (0–63, as a string key) → base64 of that 16-byte plaintext block.

200.description: Decoded tag plus any DB match

decoded.description: DecodedOpenPrintTag — normalized tag fields (materialName, brandName, materialType, color, temperatures, weightGrams, etc.).

match.description: Confident DB match (a Filament), or null when there isn't one.

candidates.description: Disambiguation candidates when the match is ambiguous.

400.description: Invalid body, missing payload/blocks, or undecodable bytes

413.description: Request body exceeds the 64 KB cap

get.summary: Match filament by instanceId or name/vendor/type
get.description: Finds the best-matching filament by instanceId (highest-confidence), exact name, vendor+type, or vendor-only. Returns a primary match and up to 5 candidates.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.description: Exact instance-ID match (case-insensitive). Unambiguous because every filament has a unique instanceId — auto-generated as 5-byte hex for new filaments, but legacy / re-imported records may carry any string. The same value NFC tags and Brother label-printer QR codes carry. When set and a match is found, the response carries that filament directly. When set but no match is found, the route falls through to name/vendor/type matching if those params are also supplied.

get.description: Returns filaments that can be used as parents for color variants (those without a parentId themselves).

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description: Filament ID to exclude (e.g. when editing)

200.description: Array of parent filaments (name, vendor, type, color, hasVariants)

color.description: Primary color hex. May be null for coextruded parents — children inherit secondaryColors via Filament.secondaryColors.

hasVariants.description: True when this filament already has ≥1 non-deleted variant. Drives the cross-hatched 'parent of variants' swatch in the picker.

post.description: Parses an uploaded INI file and returns the extracted filament profiles without saving them to the database.

400.description: No file or no profiles found

get.description: Returns all distinct filament type values (e.g. PLA, PETG, ABS).
Returns all distinct vendor names.
Returns all non-deleted nozzles, optionally filtered by diameter, type, or high-flow flag.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: Filter by nozzle diameter in mm

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by nozzle type (e.g. Brass, Hardened Steel)

200.description: Array of nozzles sorted by diameter then type

delete.description: Soft-deletes a nozzle. Blocked if any filament references it in compatibleNozzles or calibrations.

post.description: Creates a new nozzle that copies the source's spec fields (diameter, type, highFlow, hardened, notes) under a 'Name #N' suffix, with a fresh _id. Used by the printer form's move-or-clone conflict resolution. The clone is not auto-attached to any printer.

get.description: Returns all non-deleted printers with populated installed nozzles.

200.description: Array of printers sorted by manufacturer then name

delete.description: Soft-deletes a printer. Blocked if any filament calibration references it.

400.description: Cannot delete — referenced by filament calibrations

post.description: Adds a new spool subdoc to the filament. At least one of label, totalWeight, lotNumber, purchaseDate, openedDate, locationId, photoDataUrl, or retired must be supplied — an empty body returns 400 (GH #203).

label.description: Spool label (e.g. spool ID or date)

totalWeight.description: Total weight in grams (spool + filament). Null = weight unknown.

openedDate.description: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD) — when the spool was first opened

retired.description: True if the spool is empty / no longer in use; excluded from inventory totals

400.description: Empty body, invalid field type, or no recognised fields supplied

schema.description: All fields optional ($set semantics — only supplied keys change). This is the primary spool-edit caller from the /inventory page.

remainingWeight.description: Convenience input (grams of filament left, not a stored field). The server converts it to an absolute totalWeight by adding the spool's tare — the filament's spoolWeight, inherited from the parent for a variant, 0 if unset. Mutually exclusive with totalWeight (sending both → 400). PUT only; rejected on the create POST.

purchaseDate.description: ISO date string (validated; impossible calendar dates rejected — GH #372)

retired.description: Retiring a loaded spool also clears it from any printer AMS slot it occupied (side effect).

400.description: Invalid field type or value (e.g. negative weight, malformed/impossible date, rejected photo MIME)

get.description: Downloads all filaments as a CSV file with columns for name, vendor, type, color, temperatures, cost, density, weights, instance ID, drying settings, and transmission distance.
Downloads all filaments as a styled XLSX spreadsheet with auto-filter, frozen header, color-coded cells.

post.description: Upload a CSV file with header row. Requires Name, Vendor, Type columns. Only fields present are updated. The optional `Parent` column (matching the filament CSV export) attaches a newly-created filament as a variant of the named parent — honoured on create/resurrect only; ignored on update. The parent must exist among active filaments and must not itself be a variant. `Variant Count` is read-only and ignored on import.
Upload an XLSX file with header row. Same column mapping as CSV import, including the optional `Parent` column for round-tripping variant relationships.

get.description: Fetches a Prusament spool detail page and extracts specifications (material, color, temperatures, weights, pricing).

parameters.name: spoolId
parameters.description: Prusament spool ID (e.g. c6974284da) or full URL

post.description: Creates a filament from scraped Prusament data or adds a spool to an existing filament.

action.description: Create new filament or add spool to existing

get.description: Aggregates print history and direct manual spool usage for a time window.
Returns counts, inventory totals, low-stock rows, dry-cycle reminders, and recent print history for the dashboard.

get.summary: Check whether a URL can be embedded
get.description: Fetches headers for a public http(s) URL using SSRF-safe redirect handling and reports whether the URL is likely embeddable in an iframe.

400.description: Invalid JSON or duplicate bed type

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: MongoDB ObjectId of the bed type

delete.description: Soft-deletes a bed type unless it is referenced by any filament calibration.

400.description: Bed type is still referenced by a filament calibration, a printer's installed bed types, or a filament's per-bed-type temperatures

parameters.name: stats
parameters.description: When true, include spool counts and grams per location

delete.description: Soft-deletes a location unless it is referenced by any spool.

get.description: Fetches up to 8 filaments, preserving the order of the comma-separated ids query.
Exports resolved filament profiles as OrcaSlicer-compatible JSON.

post.description: Returns 501. Bulk OrcaSlicer bundle import is not yet supported; the route exists as a documented placeholder. Use POST /api/filaments/{id}/orcaslicer to sync an individual preset back, or POST /api/filaments/prusaslicer for the PrusaSlicer-INI equivalent (which IS supported).

get.summary: List distinct color name + hex pairs
get.description: Returns the deduplicated set of color-name + hex pairs in use across non-deleted filaments. Powers the color-name typeahead on the filament form so users can autocomplete previously-used color names and reuse the matching hex. Each pair appears at most once; different hexes under the same name are returned as separate entries (e.g. three brands of "Galaxy Black" with slightly different shades stay distinct).

200.description: Distinct color name + hex pairs sorted by name

name.description: The user-entered colorName saved on filaments using this hex

get.summary: Export one filament as an OrcaSlicer preset
get.description: Downloads a single filament as an OrcaSlicer filament-preset `.json` (one preset object, not the array the bundle route `/api/filaments/orcaslicer` returns). Sets a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header with a filename derived from the filament name. Variants are resolved against their parent so the preset carries full effective values.

post.description: Maps known OrcaSlicer fields back to structured filament fields and stores unknown keys in the settings bag.

get.summary: Export one filament as a PrusaSlicer config bundle
get.description: Downloads a single filament as a PrusaSlicer config bundle `.ini` (a one-filament bundle). Import via PrusaSlicer → File → Import → Import Config Bundle. Sets a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header with a filename derived from the filament name. Variants are resolved against their parent.

get.summary: Export one filament as a Bambu Studio preset
get.description: Downloads a single filament as a Bambu Studio filament-preset `.json`. Bambu Studio and OrcaSlicer share the filament-preset schema, so this reuses the OrcaSlicer generator with one Bambu-specific tweak: `from` is set to `User` so Bambu Studio files it as a user preset on import. No `inherits` base preset is set (the server can't know the user's installed system presets), so the exported preset is standalone. Variants are resolved against their parent.

post.summary: Sync a Bambu Studio preset INTO this filament (by id)
post.description: Inverse of the per-id export above. Pinned by id so the user updates exactly the filament they're looking at, even if the file's `filament_settings_id` doesn't match (renamed in the slicer, lost the link to the app's record). The parsed `name` field is intentionally ignored. Spool subdocuments, usageHistory and dryCycles are NEVER touched. Calibration values (flow ratio, pressure advance, retraction, fan speeds, max volumetric speed) auto-attach to a `calibrations[]` row tagged with the printer + nozzle inferred from `printer_settings_id` — when a printer matches but the nozzle context is ambiguous or no match exists, the response carries `calibrationUnresolved: true` so the UI can prompt the user to pick manually. Body is either `multipart/form-data` (file upload, 10 MB cap) or `application/json` with the Bambu profile directly.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: MongoDB ObjectId of the filament to update

200.description: Filament synced. Top-level fields, `bedTypeTemps[]`, settings-bag passthrough, and (when resolved) a `calibrations[]` row are all updated atomically.

calibrationApplied.description: True when a `calibrations[]` row was added or updated for the matched (printer, nozzle).

calibrationUnresolved.description: True when calibration values were present but the printer/nozzle couldn't be matched unambiguously. The top-level `maxVolumetricSpeed` still lands, but per-nozzle-only hints (flow ratio, pressure advance, retraction, fan speeds) are dropped — the UI surfaces a nudge to attach manually.

calibrationContext.description: When `calibrationApplied` is true, identifies which (printer, nozzle) the calibration row was tagged to.

settingsAdded.description: Profile keys not mapped to structured fields, preserved in the settings bag for round-trip on next export.

400.description: Malformed JSON, missing identifier, wrong content-type, oversized upload (413 on size cap), or values that fail the model's numeric validators.

403.description: Cross-origin request rejected by the trusted-origin guard.

404.description: Filament not found, OR was soft-deleted between findOne and the atomic update.

413.description: Uploaded file exceeds the 10 MB cap.

post.summary: Import a Bambu Studio preset (upsert by name)
post.description: Bulk-import companion to the per-id sync above. Matches by `filament_settings_id` (preferred — what the slicer treats as the preset name) or top-level `name`. Three-phase atomic upsert: (1) update an existing ACTIVE row, (2) resurrect a TRASHED non-purged row with the same name (clears `_deletedAt` in the same write), or (3) create new — catching E11000 from a concurrent identical import and converging on the racing winner's row. Spool subdocuments are NEVER touched. Calibration resolution is identical to the per-id variant. Body is either `multipart/form-data` (10 MB cap) or `application/json`.

200.description: Filament imported. `created` is true on the create path (or the create→update fallback after an E11000 race), false on update / resurrect.

400.description: Malformed JSON, missing identifier (`filament_settings_id`/`name`), missing `filament_type`/`filament_vendor` on create, wrong content-type, or values that fail the model's numeric validators.

403.description: Cross-origin request rejected by the trusted-origin guard.

413.description: Uploaded file exceeds the 10 MB cap.

get.description: Long-lived Server-Sent Events stream. Each NFC tag read is emitted as a `scan` event after the renderer decodes the tag and matches it against the database. The most recent scan is also replayed once on connect as a `replay` event so a slicer opened just after a tag read still picks it up. Heartbeats (`: hb`) are sent every 25 seconds to defeat idle-proxy timeouts; clients also receive a `retry: 5000` prelude so EventSource implementations reconnect after a drop. The underlying bus is in-process (Node EventEmitter on `globalThis`) — that means one Filament DB instance, not one physical machine: subscribers can be anywhere reachable over HTTP (e.g. a Pi running Filament DB driving PrusaSlicer on a Mac across the LAN). The publisher side is the only piece that pins to a machine — NFC reads come from the Electron renderer, so the reader must be plugged into whichever box runs the Electron app, and a headless Docker / web-only deploy never publishes. A horizontally-scaled multi-process deployment would need an external broker behind the bus.

parameters.name: replay
parameters.description: Set to `0` to suppress the on-connect replay of the most recent scan. Defaults to enabled.

200.description:
Server-Sent Events stream. Each record is `event: <type>
data: <json>

` where `<type>` is `scan` or `replay` and `<json>` matches the `ScanEvent` schema.

post.summary: Publish a decoded scan to subscribers
post.description: Fan-out endpoint used by the renderer's `NfcProvider` after the existing `/api/filaments/match` step. Public clients normally don't need to call this directly; documented for completeness and for testing the SSE path without a physical reader. The body is validated against an allow-list — unknown fields are stripped before the event is published, so a malformed POST cannot pollute the replay cache.

202.description: Scan accepted and emitted to all subscribers.

400.description: Body is not valid JSON, is not an object, or contains neither a filament match nor any decoded tag fields.

post.description: Decrements the target spool total weight and appends a manual usage history entry.
Appends a drying-cycle entry to a spool.

parameters.name: filamentId
parameters.description: Filter to jobs using a filament

post.description: Creates a PrintHistory row, debits referenced spools, and stamps usageHistory entries with the job id for safe undo.

get.description: Returns one PrintHistory row with the same populated fields as the list endpoint (printer name plus per-usage filament name/vendor/type/color). Tombstoned (`_deletedAt`) rows return 404.

404.description: Print history not found or already deleted

put.description: Metadata-only PUT. Accepts ONLY the five user-editable metadata fields below — usage rows + per-spool gram totals are NOT mutable here (refund + re-record via DELETE + POST to change a usage). `jobLabel` is trimmed and capped at 200 chars; `notes` is truncated to 2000 chars; `source` must be one of `manual | prusaslicer | orcaslicer | bambu | other`; `printerId` accepts `null` to clear. **Unknown body keys are REJECTED with 400** — a stray `_purged` or `_deletedAt` doesn't silently slip through. Generated clients sending legacy/extension fields like `durationSeconds` will get a 400, not a silent drop.

jobLabel.description: Trimmed; empty after trim is rejected with 400. Hard-capped at 200 chars (#350).

printerId.description: Pass `null` to clear; pass a Printer ObjectId to set.

404.description: Print history not found or already deleted

delete.description: Refunds matching spool usage and soft-deletes the PrintHistory row so sync propagates the delete tombstone.

404.description: Print history not found or already deleted

post.description: Publishes a static denormalized catalog payload for selected filaments and referenced nozzles/printers/bed types.

get.description: Returns the denormalized payload for a public shared catalog and increments its view count.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the shared catalog so peer sync propagates the unpublish tombstone.

get.description: Exports one row per spool with round-trippable columns for filament, vendor, label, total weight, lot number, dates, and location.

post.description: Bulk-creates spools from CSV. Required columns are filament and totalWeight; vendor can disambiguate, and location names are created when missing.

get.summary: Resolve a single spool by id (v1.43)
get.description: Resolve a spool's subdocument id to its owning filament (inheritance-resolved for variants) plus the spool itself. Powers the mobile scanner's spool-level deep links — the label QR's ?spool=<spoolId> link resolves here so the phone can open the filament detail with that spool highlighted without knowing the parent filament id up front.

404.description: No spool with that id (or its owning filament is trashed)

get.description: Backs the /inventory page. Returns every spool grouped by Location, with parent variant-inheritance hints surfaced so the client can compute remaining-percent without a second fetch. Retired spools are excluded by default; pass includeRetired=1 to include them. Spools whose locationId is null land in a synthetic 'no location' group sorted to the end of the response.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Filter to a single location kind (shelf, drybox, printer, ...)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter to a single filament type

parameters.name: includeRetired
parameters.description: Set to 1 to include retired spools (default: excluded)

totalSpools.description: Sum of each group's count — surfaced on the page header without re-summing client-side.

get.description: Returns the printer AMS/MMU slot the spool currently occupies, or null when unassigned. Reverse lookup over Printer.amsSlots[].spoolId.

200.description: Current assignment ({ assignment } — null when the spool is in no slot)

put.summary: Assign a spool to a printer slot
put.description: Assigns the spool to a printer AMS/MMU slot, first clearing it from any other slot — a spool occupies at most one slot at a time. Writes only Printer.amsSlots[].spoolId, never the spool's locationId. Retired spools are rejected.

400.description: Malformed body, or the spool is retired

404.description: Spool, printer, or slot not found

delete.description: Removes the spool from whatever slot it occupies. Idempotent — succeeds even if the spool was already unassigned.

get.description: Returns the OpenAPI 3.0 document, with version injected from package.json at runtime.

Downloads a JSON snapshot of core app data: filaments, nozzles, printers, bed types, locations, print history, and shared catalogs (including soft-deleted documents and tombstones). Snapshot schema version is 4 as of v1.14.0; older v1/v2/v3 snapshots still restore (missing collections come back as empty).

post.description: Restores snapshot-scoped data from a snapshot using best-effort rollback. If any part of the restore fails, the handler attempts to re-insert previous data from an in-memory backup. Concurrent restores are rejected with 409. Note: the restore is not truly atomic — concurrent readers may observe partial state during the delete/insert window, and rollback itself may fail.

delete.description: Permanently deletes filaments, nozzles, printers, bed types, locations, print history, and shared catalogs.

get.description: Exports filaments as a PrusaSlicer-compatible INI config bundle with one section per filament. Structured DB fields are mapped to PrusaSlicer INI keys and merged with the settings passthrough bag. Calibration overrides are applied dynamically via GET /api/filaments/{id}/calibration.

post.description: Imports a PrusaSlicer INI config bundle. Send raw INI text as the request body.

400.description: Empty body or no sections found

get.description: Fetches the OpenPrintTag community database from GitHub, parses material YAML files, filters to FFF filaments, and returns them with completeness scores. Cached for 1 hour. To force a refresh use POST /api/openprinttag (the old GET ?refresh=true trigger was removed — a cache-busting side effect on a GET violated REST semantics).

post.description: Force-busts the 1-hour cache and re-fetches the OpenPrintTag database from GitHub (GH #427). Replaces the removed GET ?refresh=true trigger. Same-origin guarded.

Import selected OpenPrintTag materials into Filament DB. Materials are upserted by name (not name+vendor). If a filament with the same name exists under a different vendor, the import is skipped with an error.

400.description: Missing slugs or database not loaded

get.description: Returns whether an AI API key is configured and which provider is active.

put.description: Save and validate an AI API key for TDS extraction.

delete.description: Removes the stored API key and resets provider to default.

post.description: Extract filament properties from a Technical Data Sheet URL or uploaded file using AI.
Tests connectivity to a MongoDB URI. Used during initial setup to validate the connection string.

ScanEventDecoded.description: Subset of decoded NFC tag fields useful to consumers. Allow-listed by the publish route — unknown keys are stripped.

timestamp.description: Epoch ms when the scan was published

filament.description: Matched filament, or null when no row matched

candidates.description: Plausible alternatives (vendor+type, then vendor-only) when there is no exact match

bedType.description: BedType ObjectId — pins the calibration to a specific bed surface (GH #514.5)

nozzleTemp.description: Recommended nozzle temperature in °C for this calibration

fanBridgeSpeed.description: Bridge fan speed (0–100). Round-trip pinned by PR #508.

color.description: Primary color as a hex string. May be null for coextruded multi-color filaments where no single slot is the primary — see secondaryColors. The alpha byte from the OpenPrintTag spec is not stored (translucency is modelled via finish optTags instead).

secondaryColors.description: Up to 5 additional color slots in display order, mirroring OpenPrintTag's secondary_color_0..4 keys. Coextruded filaments leave the primary null and put all colors here; gradient filaments put their stops here in feed order. Slicer-bound exports (PrusaSlicer/OrcaSlicer/Bambu Studio) drop these silently — slicer presets are single-color and the UI surfaces a notice on the export menu.

totalWeight.description: Current total weight (spool + filament) in grams

minPrintSpeed.description: Minimum recommended print speed in mm/s

maxPrintSpeed.description: Maximum recommended print speed in mm/s

spoolType.description: Spool form factor (e.g. Standard, Refill, Cardboard)

syncId.description: External sync identifier (e.g. PrusaSlicer preset name)

bedType.description: Bed surface type (e.g. Textured PEI, Cool Plate, Engineering Plate)

lowStockThreshold.description: Grams remaining across all non-retired spools below which this filament is flagged as low-stock on the inventory list + dashboard. Null disables the warning.

_deletedAt.description: Soft-delete tombstone (v1.15). Non-null means the filament is in the trash. Active filaments have null.

_purged.description: Permanent-delete tombstone (v1.15). True means the filament was purged from the trash; the row is retained only as a one-way sync signal so the deletion propagates instead of being resurrected. Stripped from all client-writable request bodies.

createdAt.description: When this spool entry was created

locationId.description: Location ObjectId — the spool's semi-permanent home (v1.11). Distinct from a printer AMS slot, which is its transient loaded position.

photoDataUrl.description: Inline data: URL of a spool photo (v1.11). SVG is rejected on write.

retired.description: Retired spools are excluded from inventory totals but keep their history (v1.11).

usageHistory.description: Per-spool filament-consumption ledger (v1.11). Entries tagged source=job/slicer mirror a PrintHistory record; source=manual/nfc are standalone.

jobId.description: PrintHistory _id for job/slicer entries; null for manual/nfc

spools.description: Individual spool entries with weights + v1.11 location/photo/retire/dry-cycle/usage metadata

inherits.description: Name of the slicer base profile this inherits from

parentId.description: Parent filament ID for color variants

settings.description: Raw slicer key-value settings (e.g. extrusion_multiplier, start_filament_gcode)

FilamentSummary.description: Lightweight projection returned by GET /api/filaments (the list view). A subset of Filament plus two computed booleans; spools[] and temperatures are reduced. NOT the full document — fetch GET /api/filaments/{id} for everything.

secondaryColors.description: Effective array — a variant with its own empty array inherits the parent's via resolveFilament's array-fallback rule.

temperatures.description: Reduced to nozzle + bed only (first-layer + other fields dropped from the list projection).

hasCalibrations.description: Computed — true when this filament OR (for a variant) its parent carries ≥1 calibration.

hasVariants.description: Computed — true when ≥1 non-deleted filament has this one as its parent.

spools.description: Reduced spool shape — only the fields the list + AMS slot picker need.

CreateFromTagInput.description: Create-from-scan variant of POST /api/filaments (mobile Phase 2, plan §4.4). The server maps the decoded tag to a filament payload, then applies overrides (which win). Same mass-assignment strips as a normal create body. When spoolRemainingGrams is sent (the scanner defaults it to the tag's net weight, since you own the roll you scanned), the server also creates ONE spool; usage history is never fabricated.

tagData.description: A DecodedOpenPrintTag exactly as POST /api/nfc/decode returns it in `decoded`. The server maps it via decodedTagToFilament. instanceId is NOT taken from the tag — it stays system-assigned (re-scans resolve via the decode route's heuristic match).

overrides.description: Filament fields that override the tag-mapped values — the scanner sends the user-confirmed name/vendor/type (required identity fields). Server-owned fields (instanceId, _purged, …) are stripped.

spoolRemainingGrams.description: Grams of filament remaining for the spool to create (the scanner defaults this to the tag's net weight). The server adds the tag tare to derive the spool's gross weight and creates ONE spool. Omit or null to create no spool (catalog-only).

color.description: Primary color as a hex string. Send null for coextruded multi-color filaments where no single slot is the primary — colors then go in secondaryColors.

secondaryColors.description: Up to 5 additional color slots in display order, mirroring OpenPrintTag's secondary_color_0..4 keys. Sending an empty array on a variant inherits the parent's array; send a non-empty array to override.

installedNozzles.description: Populated nozzle objects. Physical instances — one nozzle belongs to exactly one printer (#232).

installedBedTypes.description: Bed surfaces this printer can use (v1.23). Shared catalog — a surface like 'Textured PEI' can be referenced by many printers.

maxFlow.description: Rated max volumetric flow in mm³/s (v1.11)

enclosed.description: Whether the printer has an enclosure (affects ABS/ASA/PC compatibility) (v1.11)

autoBedLevel.description: Whether the printer has hardware auto bed levelling (v1.11)

BuildVolume.description: Build volume in mm; any axis may be null (unspecified).

AmsSlot.description: One multi-material system slot. spoolId is intentionally ref-less (a spool is a subdocument of a Filament, not a top-level collection — see GH #280); it is also nulled on cross-side hybrid-sync remap.

filamentId.description: Currently-loaded filament ObjectId; null = empty slot

spoolId.description: Currently-loaded spool subdocument id; null = no specific spool tracked

BedType.description: A bed/build-plate surface spec (v1.23). Shared catalog referenced by printers via installedBedTypes. DELETE is refused while any printer still references it.

installedNozzles.description: Array of Nozzle ObjectIds. Duplicate ids are de-duplicated at the route (GH #524.4).

installedBedTypes.description: Array of BedType ObjectIds (v1.23). Duplicates de-duplicated at the route.
